author: @tiger12506
text:
That's inappropriate way to measure power output. Short-circuit current and open-circuit voltage are two different operating conditions, You can't just multiple them together and claim a power output, you need to measure voltage and current under the *same* load, and of course, there will be the sweet spot load where the source impedance matches the load impedance. This is the fallacy that most Bedini motor researchers fall into when they claim free energy.

FWIW, I've personally rode a bicycle generator running a true 100W bulb in the past. It's a heavy workout, and I certainly wouldn't be maintaining it for very long, even if I were in shape. You personally probably already had that perspective, but I hope that provides some to your viewers out there. I think it would be definitely doable to run some LED lighting in an exercise gym...

author: @IceProducts
text:
Hi Rob, that is a nice project piece.  However, I am fairly confident you aren't generating 0.5A @ 30V.  30V is what you are getting without a load (a high impedance voltmeter) and 0.5A is what you are getting "shorting it out" and very low voltage.  To determine the actual power you would need to measure voltage and current at the same time.

Keep up the great work!

author: @MikesAllotment
text: Surely the 0.5A @ 30V is purely down to the motor you're using, and absolutely nothing to do with the fact that you're 'driving' it from an old rowing machine.  You could equally be 'driving' it from a wind turbine and get exactly the same amps and volts.  Can you please share the exact model of motor (from a window opener I think you mentioned?)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: personally I would think it would be down to the efficiency that the little set up can transfer the energy I am putting into the system into electrical energy - so it will be down to the machine and the motor and how hard I row - I am not sure what the motor is a 12v window opener - but to be honest I would think any dc perm magnet motor would be good

author: @masteragario3335
text: Hi. I did the same except I kept it simpler than your good self. I ended up keeping the air resistance wheel to keep a higher gear ratio, I also kept the flywheel to help keep momentum on the back stroke. I'm not a motor man and threw an old ford fiesta alternator on it, it worked but not great. What would you recommend for a generator and any ideas where to get belts, my adhoc ghetto gaffer tape belt is not ideal.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: car alternators work great once you get the rpm over around 2-3,000 below that they are not so good - that's because they are really synchronous generators - a perm magnet dc motor used as a generator I think is a better option for low rpm applications

Now to get more Watts out of it connect the output to rectifier and rectifier to a 16V (low rpm, high torque)) or 30V (high rpm low torque)battery and your Watts output should go up (at the calories cost of course ;) ) :D Best regards! As always great project!

author: @AtlasReburdened
text: With a decently fit user and properly matched generator, one can pretty easily maintain an 80~100W output for over 30 minutes using a rowing motion, and I can personally attest to being able to produce peak outputs in excess of 450W, though only for 20 seconds or so.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: in principle this is a setup that works that's for sure

replies.author: @AtlasReburdened
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering More than in principle. I repaired gym equipment for a few years and occasionally came across row machines that used the rectified output of low rpm three phase generators, put across a high power shunt resistor to give the user real measurements of their efforts based on the voltage drop. I used my lunch break once to quantify what I could maintain on a nice one that a college had in their gym, and was able to pull 100W for the duration without too much exertion. On a separate occasion at the same place, I just went for maximum effort and exceeded 450W.

text: Fantastic. I expect that air brake wheel could be used as a blade for a wind turbine setup !!!

replies.author: @edkoetsier339
replies.text: Wind turbine blade power capture is directly proportional to swept area. That thing has very little. Wouldn't even turn.

replies.author: @errolmagill1611
replies.text: @edkoetsier339 👍. I wouldn't mind experimenting, I have seen small ones turn car alternators before.

replies.author: @edkoetsier339
replies.text: @errolmagill1611 Test away. You will be disappointed.

replies.author: @Barskor1
replies.text: @edkoetsier339 Air dams like water dams so rather than working with swept areas and massive blades whirling about it all gets channeled to one spot and wiz-bang done.
